name: madara
description: 斑 — the coats that pattern themselves, born from nothing. Turing asked, in his last paper, how a featureless egg becomes a spotted leopard. The answer is two chemicals chasing each other: a Gray–Scott reaction–diffusion in which one substance diffuses, feeds, and another devours it and decays. From a plain field plus a few sparks, spots, stripes, labyrinths and holes arise on their own — and the skin names the creature whose coat it grew. Deterministic from a seed; the core knows neither DOM nor network.
